concept.text: Some sounds are loud, like a drum! Some sounds are soft, like a whisper.
concept.funFact: Music is made of loud and soft sounds together!

concept.text: Drums make a beat! Tap-tap-tap. You can clap with the beat too!
concept.funFact: Some drums are so big you have to stand to play them!

questions.prompt: How do you play a drum?
Can you clap to a beat?

concept.text: When we sing, we use our voice to make music. Singing can be happy or sad!
concept.funFact: Babies start to sing before they learn to talk!

concept.text: Some sounds are HIGH like a tweeting bird. Some sounds are LOW like a big bear's growl!
concept.funFact: A piano can play both very high and very low sounds!

questions.prompt: Can a piano play high and low?

concept.text: When music plays, our bodies want to move! That's called dancing. It's fun!
concept.funFact: People all over the world dance to music!

questions.prompt: When we move to music we are...

concept.text: Instruments come in families: strings (guitar, violin), woodwinds (flute, clarinet), brass (trumpet, trombone), and percussion (drums, xylophone).
concept.funFact: The violin has only 4 strings but can play hundreds of different notes!

concept.text: The beat is the steady pulse in music — like a heartbeat. Rhythm is the pattern of long and short sounds played over the beat.
concept.funFact: Marching bands all walk to the beat so they stay together!

questions.prompt: The pattern of long and short sounds is called ____.
Marching bands stay together by following the...

concept.text: Composers write music. Mozart was a child genius who composed his first piece at age 5. Beethoven kept composing even after he became deaf. Bach wrote beautiful, complex music.
concept.funFact: Beethoven wrote his famous 9th Symphony when he could no longer hear!

questions.prompt: Mozart wrote his first piece at age ____.

concept.text: Music has many styles called genres: rock, jazz, classical, hip-hop, country, pop, and reggae. Each has its own sound and history.
concept.funFact: Jazz was born in New Orleans, USA over 100 years ago!

questions.prompt: A style of music is called a ____.
Which is NOT a music genre?

concept.text: Music is written on lines and spaces called a staff. Notes have names: A, B, C, D, E, F, G. They repeat over and over!
concept.funFact: Music uses just 7 letters to make endless songs!

questions.prompt: How many letters are music notes?

concept.text: A scale is a series of notes that sound good together. The major scale (do-re-mi-fa-sol-la-ti-do) has a happy sound. The minor scale sounds more sad or mysterious.
concept.funFact: The 'Happy Birthday' song uses the major scale!

questions.prompt: A minor scale often sounds sad.
A series of notes that sound good together is called a ____.
The syllable that starts the major scale is...
Order the syllables of the major scale.

concept.text: An orchestra has 4 sections: strings (most numerous), woodwinds, brass, and percussion. A conductor leads them. Famous orchestral works include Beethoven's 5th Symphony and Vivaldi's Four Seasons.
concept.funFact: A modern orchestra can have over 100 musicians!

questions.prompt: The most numerous section is the ____ section.

concept.text: Different cultures have unique music: Brazilian samba, Indian classical raga, Japanese taiko drumming, Caribbean reggae, and African djembe drumming. Each tells a cultural story.
concept.funFact: The djembe drum is over 800 years old and originated in West Africa!

questions.prompt: Reggae music originated in the Caribbean.

concept.text: Sound travels in waves. High notes have fast (high-frequency) vibrations; low notes have slow ones. Loudness (amplitude) is how big the waves are.
concept.funFact: The human ear can hear sounds from about 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz!

questions.prompt: How loud a sound is is called ____.
Humans can hear up to about...

concept.text: Western music has eras: Baroque (Bach, Vivaldi), Classical (Mozart, Haydn), Romantic (Beethoven, Chopin), and Modern. Each era has unique style and instruments.
concept.funFact: The Baroque era ended around 1750 — the year Bach died.

questions.prompt: Which composer is from the Baroque era?
Order the eras from earliest to latest.
Beethoven is considered a Romantic-era composer.
